name: Israel
headline: Israel FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — May 3 war-resumption-threat-to-force-disarmament documented as new coercive-diplomacy signal. Ceasefire fraying under acute strain: aid trucks averaging below agreed 600/day threshold; 800+ killed since October ceasefire; 8 aid workers killed; OCHA 6-month review documents humanitarian situation 'far from over'; negotiations stalled. Mixed signal: OCHA 6-month review also documents famine averted and 1.1M people reached monthly — these are limited positive sub-anchor signals attributable to the international aid system, NOT to Israeli policy, so they do not move the dimensional scoring. Hungary ICC reversal (Magyar government enforces warrants) remains active external INT signal narrowing Israeli leadership travel footprint. Floor sustained at 0.0; ceasefire-fraying documentation added; coercive-diplomacy signal documented as new sub-category.

AWR.rationale: Floor sustained. State-level recognition of civilian harm absent or rhetorically inverted; OCHA primary-source documentation continues despite operational obstruction.

EMP.rationale: Floor sustained. 800+ killed during ceasefire and 8 aid workers killed represent EMP-dimension failure even under nominal ceasefire.

ACT.rationale: Floor sustained. AC1 responsiveness: aid trucks averaging below agreed 600/day threshold demonstrates non-implementation of ceasefire-agreed actions; AC2 proportionality: May 3 war-resumption threat is disproportionate response framing for disarmament-enforcement purpose.

EQU.rationale: Floor sustained. Settler violence in West Bank with state non-enforcement (canonical floor evidence) continues; aid-trucks-below-threshold falls disproportionately on civilians.

BND.rationale: Floor sustained. B1 consent: continued unilateral threat of war resumption violates ceasefire consent framework; B3 scope: aid worker deaths during ceasefire show scope expansion.

ACC.rationale: Floor sustained. ICJ provisional measures, ICC arrest warrants for Netanyahu and Gallant unaddressed (canonical). Hungary ICC reversal under Magyar government is external integrity signal narrowing travel footprint.

SYS.rationale: Floor sustained. S1 causal reasoning: coercive-diplomacy framing (war resumption as disarmament tool) is a systems-level commitment to military-leverage doctrine; S2 long-term impact: 60,000+ civilian casualties documented (canonical), structural settler violence.

INT.rationale: Floor sustained. I1 consistency under pressure: war-resumption-threat-to-force-disarmament is coercive-diplomacy under ceasefire — a structurally inconsistent posture. External accountability signal (Hungary ICC reversal) operates against this entity's integrity profile.

evidence.summary: Al Jazeera May 3 2026: Israel threatens Gaza war resumption to force Hamas disarmament; ceasefire frays.
OCHA six months into the ceasefire: humanitarian aid has saved lives in Gaza but risks remain — primary-source document covering full ceasefire period.
UNSC press briefing on Gaza situation; 800+ killed since ceasefire start, 8 aid workers killed; aid trucks averaging below 600/day threshold.

keyEvidence: May 3 2026: Israel threatened to resume war in Gaza to force Hamas disarmament — new coercive-diplomacy signal.
800+ killed since October ceasefire; 8 aid workers killed; aid trucks averaging below agreed 600/day threshold.
OCHA 6-month ceasefire review: humanitarian situation 'far from over'; famine averted and 1.1M people reached monthly attributable to international aid system (NOT to Israeli policy).
Hungary ICC reversal under Magyar government — external integrity signal narrowing Israeli leadership travel footprint (carry-over from May 13).

Canonical floor designation evidence: ICJ provisional measures (Jan 26 2024, revised May 24 2024); ICC arrest warrants for Netanyahu and Gallant (Nov 21 2024); IPC famine determinations; 60,000+ documented civilian casualties; structural settler violence; press obstruction (150+ journalists killed).
rationale: Floor sustained. May 14 cycle adds two documentation categories: (a) coercive-diplomacy signal (May 3 war-resumption-threat-to-force-disarmament); (b) ceasefire-fraying-with-systematic-non-implementation (aid trucks below threshold, 800+ killed). The mixed-signal handling is important — OCHA's documentation of famine averted and 1.1M reached monthly is attributable to international aid system effectiveness, NOT to Israeli policy or behavior; the positive signal therefore does not flow to Israel's dimensional scoring. Hungary ICC reversal external signal continues to operate. Floor-exit criteria not in trajectory.
watches: UNSC further action on aid-worker deaths
carryForward: MSF manufactured-malnutrition crisis (May 13 cycle: 50% pregnant women malnourished, 90% babies premature, 84% low birth weight)
Hungary ICC reversal as external accountability signal (May 13 cycle)
ICJ provisional measures + ICC arrest warrants (carry-over)
newConductCategoryNote: The May 3 war-resumption-threat-to-force-disarmament is a coercive-diplomacy sub-category distinct from prior ceasefire-violation documentation. Methodologically: coercive-diplomacy-under-ceasefire is a structurally inconsistent posture (B1 consent + I1 consistency dual failure) that should be tracked as a distinct conduct sub-category in the floor cluster.

math_check.validator_check: Floor maintained. Positive sub-anchor signals (OCHA famine-averted, 1.1M reached monthly) are attributed to international aid system, not Israeli policy — do not flow to Israel's dimensional scoring.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
